role_summary: Civil rights movement participant; in this corpus's embedding her testimony pairs with Gloria Claudette Grinnell, Junius Williams, Joan Trumpauer Mulholland, and Esther M. A. Terry.
ai_reading: George's top semantic neighbors are Gloria Claudette Grinnell (entry 48, count 83), Junius Williams (entry 72, Newark Black Power Conference), Joan Trumpauer Mulholland (entry 60, Freedom Rider), Esther M. A. Terry (entry 40), and the John Dudley joint interview (entry 64). The cluster is heterogeneous, spanning Freedom Rides, SNCC, and Newark Black Power organizing. Her +0.66 reading on the Nonviolence as Theology vs Armed Self-Defense axis places her on the Self-Defense side, continuing the catalog's pattern of SCLC-and-direct-action figures landing opposite their cultural placement on that axis.
biographical_paragraph: Virginia Simms George's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 122 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Independent biographical sourcing is pending; the strongest signal currently available for movement role is the embedding pattern visible in the AI's reading above.
